summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined strike on Ukraine overnight with 2 cruise missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts recorded at 11 locations. Russian MoD claims liberation of Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, plus destruction of two Ukrainian MiG-29s at an airfield. Ukraine's Constitution Day was marked by political developments including Trump signaling possible revision of the 'Anchorage agreements' on Ukraine at the G7 summit.
key_points: Russia struck Ukraine overnight with 2 ship-launched cruise missiles (Zircon/Oniks), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones, with hits recorded at 11 locations and debris at 13 others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
Russian MoD reported liberation of Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Russian MoD claimed two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft were destroyed at an airfield. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)
Russian MoD reported a Geran strike on a training camp of the operational center of special operations 'Yuzh' of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in Mykolaiv Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382745)

Trump at the G7 summit raised the possibility of revising the 'Anchorage agreements,' indicating he may abandon prior US agreement that Donbas remain under Russian control in any peace deal. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215)
Ukrainian long-range strikes hit two Russian oil refineries: Slovyansky in Krasnodar region (300 km from front line) and one in Yaroslavl region (700 km from border). (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)

Ukraine's 11th Army Corps spokesman stated the Slavyansk-Izyum highway may soon be closed for safe passage; Bloomberg reports Ukrainian positions in Donetsk continue to deteriorate. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31210)

At the Ukraine Recovery Conference (URC2026), international partners announced over 550 million euros in support for energy sector preparation ahead of the next heating season. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23599)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined overnight strike on Ukraine employing 2 cruise missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations. Russian MoD claims territorial gains in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts alongside strikes on Ukrainian military infrastructure. The political dimension intensified as Trump signaled possible revision of the 'Anchorage agreements' at the G7 summit, while Ukraine continued long-range strikes against Russian rear-area oil refineries.

points: Ukraine's Air Force reported that the overnight Russian strike included 2 anti-ship cruise missiles (Zircon/Oniks), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones, with one missile and 14 strike UAVs hitting 11 locations and debris falling at 13 additional locations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)

Ukrainian long-range strikes hit the Slovyansky oil refinery in Krasnodar region (approximately 300 km from the front line) and a refinery in Yaroslavl region (approximately 700 km from the border), according to President Zelensky. (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)

The Slavyansk-Izyum highway may soon be closed to safe passage, according to Dmitry Zaporozhets, spokesman for the Ukrainian 11th Army Corps, as positions in Donetsk region continue to deteriorate. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31210)
Footage from Lutsk showed a man being pushed into a van during mobilization efforts; city residents attempted to free him but were unsuccessful. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31220)

At URC2026, international partners pledged over 550 million euros for Ukraine's energy sector preparation, including 175 million USD from the US, 137 million euros from Sweden, 77 million euros from Norway, and 178 million euros from the Netherlands. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23599)
The Ukrainian Defense Forces' FOBOS unit of the 46th Airmobile Brigade released footage of ongoing operations against Russian forces. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23600)

In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, nearly 20 enemy attacks with drones and artillery were recorded; three gas stations were burning and a sports school was damaged, with two people injured according to the regional administration. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233)
Russian MoD reported that Russian Armed Forces liberated Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Russian MoD claimed a Geran drone strike on a training camp of the operational center of special operations 'Yuzh' of the Ukrainian Armed Forces in Mykolaiv Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382745)
Russian MoD reported destruction of two Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft at an airfield. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)

President Putin, at the United Russia party congress, stated Russia cannot be defeated on the battlefield and accused the Kyiv regime of resorting to terrorist actions while retreating along the entire line of contact. (Source: @tass_agency/382782)

Putin declared that the West cannot defeat Russia militarily and is attempting to destabilize the political situation, while asserting Russia's strategic plans will be fully implemented. (Source: @tass_agency/382782)
Chinese state television aired a segment on Russian strikes on Ukraine but used footage of a burning Moscow oil refinery as the visual accompaniment. (Source: @eRadarrua/98483)

Ukrainian positions in Donetsk region continue to deteriorate with Russian troops advancing closer to Kramatorsk and Slavyansk, according to Bloomberg citing military analyst Michael Kofman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31214)
The Slavyansk-Izyum highway may soon be closed for safe passage per the 11th Army Corps spokesman, threatening a key logistics artery in the Donetsk direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31210)
Russian MoD claimed liberation of Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, an area adjacent to the eastern front. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Drones were tracked from Sumy Oblast toward Chernihiv Oblast in the direction of Novhorod-Siverskyi, with additional drones moving southward in Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66659)
In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, nearly 20 enemy attacks with drones and artillery were recorded in a single day, targeting infrastructure including gas stations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82233)
A ballistic missile was tracked heading toward Bila Tserkva in Kyiv Oblast, with air defense systems active. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68103)
Multiple ballistic missile threats were declared for Kyiv, with warnings of possible repeated strikes on the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68134)
Drones were detected near Kulykivka in Chernihiv Oblast, later confirmed as a weather balloon. (Source: @eRadarrua/98484)
Drones from Sumy Oblast were tracked toward Poltavska Oblast, passing near Zinkiv. (Source: @kpszsu/66642)
A reactive-type drone was reported over Chernihiv Oblast in the Chornobyl zone area heading toward Kyiv Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98478)
Russian MoD reported liberation of Novoselivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)
Russian MoD claimed a Geran strike on the special operations 'Yuzh' training camp in Mykolaiv Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382745)
Eight people were reported injured in a Russian attack on Zaporizhzhia, including two children, with a five-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)
Drones were tracked in southern Mykolaiv Oblast heading north, and additional drones were moving toward Zaporizhzhia from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66636)
Two MiG-29 aircraft were reportedly destroyed at an airfield according to Russian MoD claims. (Source: @tass_agency/382749)
Russia launched 2 anti-ship cruise missiles (Zircon/Oniks), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones in the overnight strike. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
One missile and 14 strike drones hit 11 locations; debris from intercepted targets fell at 13 additional locations across Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
Possible launches of Banderol-type missile drones from a northern direction were reported. (Source: @eRadarrua/98497)
A ballistic missile threat to Kyiv was declared twice, with the first missile entering Ukrainian airspace. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68142)
Air alert was lifted in Kyiv at 03:11 local time on June 28, 2026. (Source: @eRadarrua/98466)
Drones targeted multiple oblasts including Dnipropetrovsk (3 drones near Yurivka), Dnipro city, Poltava Oblast (Dikanka district), Chernihiv, and Sumy. (Source: @eRadarrua/98510)

key_developments: Russia conducted a massive combined overnight strike with 2 cruise missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, hitting 11 locations across Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)

Trump at the G7 summit indicated possible revision of the 'Anchorage agreements,' signaling potential US shift from prior acceptance of Russian control of Donbas in any peace deal. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215)
Ukrainian strikes hit two Russian oil refineries — Slovyansky in Krasnodar (300 km from front) and one in Yaroslavl region (700 km from border). (Source: @UkraineNow/82237)
Russian MoD claims liberation of Pysantsy (Dnipropetrovsk Oblast) and Novoselivka (Zaporizhzhia Oblast) along the eastern and southern front. (Source: @tass_agency/382744)

The Slavyansk-Izyum highway faces imminent closure per the 11th Army Corps, threatening Ukrainian logistics in Donetsk as Russian forces approach Kramatorsk and Slavyansk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31210)
International partners at URC2026 pledged over 550 million euros for Ukraine's energy sector, with the US alone committing 175 million USD. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23599)
Russian MoD reported a Geran strike on the 'Yuzh' special operations training camp in Mykolaiv Oblast and destruction of two MiG-29s at an airfield. (Source: @tass_agency/382745)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is intensifying as Russia demonstrates continued capacity for large-scale combined-arms strikes employing cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, and massed drone attacks across multiple oblasts simultaneously. The political dimension presents a significant shift: Trump's signal at the G7 that the US may abandon the 'Anchorage agreements' — which had conceded Russian control of Donbas — introduces strategic uncertainty for Ukraine's diplomatic position. On the ground, the potential closure of the Slavyansk-Iyzum highway threatens Ukrainian logistics in Donetsk, where Russian forces are pressing toward Kramatorsk and Slavyansk. Ukraine's continued ability to strike Russian rear-area infrastructure, demonstrated by refinery hits 300-700 km from the front, provides a counter-pressure capability but does not offset the deteriorating ground situation in Donetsk.
sections.regional_response: The G7 summit emerged as a critical diplomatic venue where Trump raised the possibility of revising the 'Anchorage agreements,' indicating potential US willingness to pressure Russia harder on the Donbas question. International support for Ukraine's energy infrastructure solidified at URC2026 with over 550 million euros pledged, led by the US (175 million USD), Sweden (137 million euros), the Netherlands (178 million euros), and Norway (77 million euros). The UK confirmed state guarantees for a 210 million pound loan for nuclear fuel supplies via Urenco. China's state broadcaster inadvertently revealed domestic sensitivity to the conflict by using footage of a burning Moscow refinery to illustrate Russian strikes on Ukraine. Putin's United Russia congress address framed the conflict as a test of Russian resilience against a West that cannot defeat Russia militarily.
sections.intelligence_notes: Several information gaps require monitoring: the exact type and origin of the ballistic missile targeting Kyiv remains unconfirmed; the claimed liberation of Pysantsy and Novoselivka requires independent verification; and the true impact of the Geran strike on the 'Yuzh' training camp in Mykolaiv Oblast is unclear. The reported Banderol-type missile drone launches from a northern direction represent a potential new threat vector requiring identification. The deterioration of Ukrainian positions in Donetsk, as reported by Bloomberg and the 11th Army Corps spokesman, appears to be the most significant operational trend, yet detailed frontline control changes remain unverified. The Chinese state media error in using Moscow refinery footage may indicate either production sloppiness or deliberate signaling regarding the domestic impact of Ukrainian strikes on Russian territory.
